chunk_summary: This section outlines antibiotic regimens and micronutrient dosing protocols for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing infection treatment, weight-based dosing, and stabilization before iron supplementation.
learning_objectives: Understand the antibiotic regimens for uncomplicated and complicated SAM, including septic shock.
Learn the micronutrient dosing protocols for stabilization in SAM management.
Recognize the importance of addressing infections and micronutrient deficiencies in SAM treatment.
Identify when to initiate iron supplementation during SAM recovery.
key_facts: Oral amoxicillin 15 mg/kg every 8 hours for 5 days is recommended for uncomplicated SAM.
For complicated SAM, injectable ampicillin 50 mg/kg every 6 hours plus gentamicin 7.5 mg/kg once daily for 7 days is advised.
Septic shock may require cefotaxime 50 mg/kg every 6 hours or ceftriaxone 100 mg/kg/day.
Micronutrient therapy includes vitamin A, folic acid (5 mg on day 1, then 1 mg/day), zinc (2 mg/kg/day), and copper (0.3 mg/kg/day).
Iron supplementation (3 mg/kg/day) should begin only after stabilization and infection control.
Infections and micronutrient deficiencies are key drivers of anorexia and non-response in SAM.
reference_quote: Antibiotic regimens should be expressed in weight-based doses to avoid unsafe copying. Facility-based operational guidance describes the following commonly used regimens: for uncomplicated SAM managed within the protocol, oral amoxicillin 15 mg/kg per dose every 8 hours for 5 days.
document_key_topics: Stabilization and infection control in SAM
